summary: The IDF has intensified operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, destroying a Hezbollah artillery command and conducting a large‑scale ground offensive on the Bofar ridge and Nahal Saluki. Multiple rocket and drone attacks from Lebanon have been intercepted, while civilian protection measures were heightened in northern Israel. Iran announced a new drone support centre, and a hospital in Nahariya moved patients to an underground floor.
key_points: The IDF destroyed a Hezbollah‑operated artillery command in southern Lebanon, finding secondary explosives indicating weapons inside the building (Source: @idf_telegram/23989).
Several rocket launches from Lebanon crossed into Israel; some were intercepted and others fell in open areas with no casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/23991).
A broad offensive began on the Bofar ridge and Nahal Saluki involving the Golani, 7th, Givati, Fire Brigades, and a multi‑dimensional unit under the 36th Division (Source: @idf_telegram/24001).
Alerts were issued for a hostile aircraft that entered Israeli airspace from Lebanon and was subsequently neutralised (Source: @New_security8200/200377).
Civil defence upgraded red‑zone guidance for multiple northern communities, restricting educational activities and limiting public gatherings (Source: @New_security8200/200361).
Nahariya’s Galilee Medical Center moved patients to an underground floor following updated civil defence instructions (Source: @New_security8200/200376).
Hezbollah launched a drone that struck a military zone near Shomera; another drone was intercepted with no injuries reported (Source: @manniefabian/58315).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF destroyed a Hezbollah artillery command in southern Lebanon and launched a large ground operation on the Bofar ridge and Nahal Saluki, involving multiple brigades. Rocket and drone attacks from Lebanon have been intercepted, prompting heightened civil defence alerts across northern Israel. Iran announced a new drone support centre, while civilian infrastructure in Nahariya was reinforced underground.

points: Iran’s Ministry of Defense announced the imminent establishment of a drone support centre to serve civilian and military drone operations (Source: @presstv/192034).
The IDF destroyed a Hezbollah‑operated artillery command in southern Lebanon, identifying secondary explosives that indicated weapons inside the structure (Source: @idf_telegram/23989).

A broad offensive began on the Bofar ridge and Nahal Saluki involving the Golani, 7th, Givati, Fire Brigades, and a multi‑dimensional unit under the 36th Division, aiming to eliminate Hezbollah infrastructure (Source: @idf_telegram/24001).
Multiple rocket launches from Lebanon crossed into Israel; some were intercepted and others fell in open areas with no casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/23991).
Hezbollah launched a drone that struck a military zone near Shomera; another drone was intercepted with no injuries reported (Source: @manniefabian/58315).
Civil defence upgraded red‑zone guidance for northern communities, restricting educational activities and limiting public gatherings (Source: @New_security8200/200361).
Nahariya’s Galilee Medical Center moved patients to an underground floor following updated civil defence instructions (Source: @New_security8200/200376).
Hezbollah fired rockets from Lebanon into Israel; some were intercepted while others landed in open areas without causing casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/23991).
A hostile aircraft entered Israeli airspace from Lebanon and was neutralised after detection (Source: @New_security8200/200377).
Hezbollah deployed a drone that struck near Shomera, prompting sirens; a second drone was intercepted with no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58315).

key_developments: The IDF destroyed a Hezbollah‑operated artillery command in southern Lebanon, finding secondary explosives indicating weapons inside the building (Source: @idf_telegram/23989).
Several rocket launches from Lebanon crossed into Israel; some were intercepted and others fell in open areas with no casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/23991).
A broad offensive began on the Bofar ridge and Nahal Saluki involving the Golani, 7th, Givati, Fire Brigades, and a multi‑dimensional unit under the 36th Division (Source: @idf_telegram/24001).
Alerts were issued for a hostile aircraft that entered Israeli airspace from Lebanon and was subsequently neutralised (Source: @New_security8200/200377).
Civil defence upgraded red‑zone guidance for multiple northern communities, restricting educational activities and limiting public gatherings (Source: @New_security8200/200361).
Nahariya’s Galilee Medical Center moved patients to an underground floor following updated civil defence instructions (Source: @New_security8200/200376).
Hezbollah launched a drone that struck a military zone near Shomera; another drone was intercepted with no injuries reported (Source: @manniefabian/58315).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to employ rockets and drones from southern Lebanon, targeting northern Israeli communities and military zones, indicating an escalation in low‑intensity fire. The IDF’s large‑scale ground operation on the Bofar ridge and Nahal Saluki reflects a shift toward deeper penetration to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure. Iran’s establishment of a drone support centre may enhance its own aerial capabilities, potentially influencing proxy activities in the region.
sections.regional_response: Israel has responded with intensified air and ground strikes, expanded civil defence alerts, and restrictions on civilian activities in the affected zones. Hezbollah’s continued attacks and the presence of hostile aircraft suggest a sustained proxy conflict, while Iran’s drone centre could embolden its allies. International observers are likely monitoring the escalation, but no direct foreign intervention is reported in the sources.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of rocket and drone launch sites in southern Lebanon should be prioritized to pre‑empt further incursions. The red‑zone civil defence adjustments indicate heightened risk to civilian infrastructure in northern Israel. Iran’s new drone hub may lead to increased UAV activity in the broader theater, warranting surveillance of Iranian‑linked air assets.
